13	# The augmented Čech complex is exact (blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`)
14	
15	This file proves that the augmented Čech complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is exact
16	(every homology object is the zero object in `X.Modules`) whenever `𝒰` is a finite
17	affine open cover of `X` with all pairwise intersections affine (ensured by separatedness
18	of `X`) and `F` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module.
19	
20	The theorem is placed here rather than in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` to avoid an import
21	cycle: every ingredient of the sections/sheafification proof route
22	(`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`, `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`,
23	`affineCoverSystem`, `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`) lives in a file that transitively imports
24	`CechHigherDirectImage.lean`.  Placing `cechAugmented_exact` in this downstream file
25	(`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`) makes all those ingredients available without a cycle.

27	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero`, `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`)
28	live in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` and are importable here.

45	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — reflecting `IsZero` through a faithful functor -/
46	
47	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
48	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then
49	`X` is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` +
50	`Functor.map_injective`; lighter than `reflects_exact_of_faithful`, which needs
51	`[Abelian]`.) Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory). -/
52	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
53	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [HasZeroMorphisms C] [HasZeroMorphisms D]
54	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
55	    (h : IsZero (F.obj Y)) : IsZero Y := by
56	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
57	  apply F.map_injective
58	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
59	  exact h

61	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — a null-homotopic identity kills homology -/
62	
63	/-- **A complex homotopy-equivalent to zero has vanishing homology.**
64	If the identity of a homological complex `D` is homotopic to the zero map
65	(`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`), then every homology object `D.homology p` is a zero object.
66	This is the categorical packaging of the contracting-homotopy criterion: the
67	prepend-`i_fix` homotopy on the section {\v C}ech complex produces exactly such a
68	`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`, and this lemma converts it into the required
69	`IsZero (D.homology p)`.
70	
71	The proof is the standard three-lemma combo: homotopy-invariance of the induced
72	homology map (`Homotopy.homologyMap_eq`), the identity/zero computations
73	(`homologyMap_id`, `homologyMap_zero`), and the "identity equals zero" criterion for a
74	zero object (`IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero`).  Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological
75	algebra); blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`, formalization mechanism Step 3(d). -/
76	lemma isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
77	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (D : HomologicalComplex C c) (p : ι)
78	    [D.HasHomology p] (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) := by
79	  refine (IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero _).mpr ?_
80	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_id, ho.homologyMap_eq p, HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_zero]
81	
82	/-- **Transport `IsZero` of homology across a complex iso plus a null-homotopic identity.**
83	If `D ≅ D'` as homological complexes and the identity of `D'` is homotopic to `0`, then
84	`D.homology p` is a zero object.  This is the consumer-side glue for the section-{\v C}ech
85	route: the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech complex `D` is identified with a concrete section
86	complex `D'` (`cechSection_complex_iso`) which carries a contracting homotopy
87	(`cechSection_contractible`); this lemma turns that pair into `IsZero (D.homology p)`.
88	Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological algebra). -/
89	lemma isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
90	    [CategoryWithHomology C] {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι}
91	    {D D' : HomologicalComplex C c} (e : D ≅ D') (p : ι)
92	    (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) :=
93	  IsZero.of_iso (isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero D' p ho)
94	    ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor C c p).mapIso e)

96	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a locally-zero presheaf -/
97	
98	open Opposite in
99	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf that is locally a zero object vanishes.**
100	If for every object `U` of the site there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U` all of whose
101	members `g : V ⟶ U` land on an object `V` where `Q.obj (op V)` is a zero object, then
102	the sheafification of `Q` is the zero object.
103	
104	This packages the three imported site lemmas
105	(`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`) into the "locally zero" form the
106	{\v C}ech-resolution argument consumes: the presheaf homology `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V, F)` is a zero
107	object over every `V` contained in a cover member, and those `V` are a covering basis.
108	The local injectivity of `0 : Q ⟶ Z` (`Z` the constant zero presheaf) comes from the
109	hypothesis (each member's section group is a subsingleton); the local surjectivity is
110	free (the target is a zero object). Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
111	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C]
112	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
113	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
114	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}
115	    (h : ∀ U : C, ∃ S : Sieve U, S ∈ J U ∧
116	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → IsZero (Q.obj (op V))) :
117	    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}).obj Q) := by

- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
- **List every new declaration you introduce under a `## Needs blueprint entry` heading in your task_result** (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) — see Logging below. The project keeps a 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence: the review agent and planner consume this list (and `archon dag-query unmatched`) to give each new declaration a blueprint block. An unreported helper is an invisible, isolated dependency that silently corrupts the frontier — flagging it is mandatory, not optional. (You never write the blueprint yourself; you only name what needs one.)
- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is display-level only. Default to `change` when in doubt.

## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md

The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:

- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.

## LSP MCP tools

The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (`mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, etc.). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, …) — same tools, never shell binaries.

- Always invoke through your tool-call interface.
- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` — there is no such shell command.
- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If `success: false`, retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.

## Search protocol

1. `lean_local_search` first.
2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.

## Tooling traps

- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading. Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors. Rely on `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):

```markdown
# Algebra/WLocal.lean

## wLocal_iff (line 45)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance
- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing

### Attempt 2
- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
- **Result:** RESOLVED
- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap

## Needs blueprint entry
- `Algebra.WLocal.helper_bijective` (line 78) — new helper, no blueprint block yet. Uses: `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective`. Reviewer/planner: add a `\label` + `\lean` + `\uses` entry so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
```

One section per theorem/lemma. Each attempt: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results. **Always include the `## Needs blueprint entry` section when you added any new declaration** (omit it only if you added none) — it is how the loop keeps Lean ↔ blueprint at 1-to-1.

## End-of-session handoff

**Before declaring done, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote down as a comment, TODO, or "possible route"? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there other open `sorry`s in my file I could attempt with the tools and lemmas I just developed?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before filing "Infrastructure missing"?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped because "it would take too long" or "the planner should decide"? If it's in my file's scope, attempt it.

Only proceed to write the task result when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable."

**Process lifetime discipline:** A prover lane stops by writing `task_results/<your_file>.md` and exiting normally. Do NOT run `pkill`, `killall`, `kill`, regex process cleanup, or any command intended to terminate Lean/Lake/shell/agent processes. Do not launch final verification in the background and then clean it up. Verification must be a blocking command whose result you wait for, or a clearly reported unfinished command. The runner/orchestrator owns cleanup after your report.

Before writing results:

4. **Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest; the planner reads this. **Only Lean code changes count as progress** — a sorry closed, a helper lemma that compiles, a partial tactic block at a specific stuck point. Comments, docstrings, TODO notes, and task result prose do NOT count. If you wrote approaches in comments that you didn't attempt, say so explicitly:
   - `Real progress`: closed N sorries — name each one. State the sorry count before and after.
   - `Partial progress`: made measurable code progress (decomposed into sub-lemmas with proof bodies, closed one branch of a case split) but did not fully close. Name the specific advance and the specific remaining blocker. Decomposing a `sorry` into N named `sorry`s with only comments in their bodies is NOT partial progress unless the bodies contain genuine proof attempts.
   - `Cosmetics only`: changed formatting/comments/style with no proof progress — say so explicitly. **Renaming a `sorry` to a named helper lemma, adding a docstring, or extracting it to a separate declaration with a `sorry` body is `Cosmetics only` unless the body contains a genuine proof attempt.**
   - `Approaches written but not attempted`: you identified routes (in comments, TODOs, or log prose) but did not attempt them — name each one and why you stopped before trying. This is a valid but weak verdict; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
   - `Avoided the goal`: attempted something adjacent but not the assigned target — explain why and what you tried instead.

14	# The augmented Čech complex is exact (blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`)
15	
16	This file proves that the augmented Čech complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is exact
17	(every homology object is the zero object in `X.Modules`) whenever `𝒰` is a finite
18	affine open cover of `X` with all pairwise intersections affine (ensured by separatedness
19	of `X`) and `F` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module.
20	
21	The theorem is placed here rather than in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` to avoid an import
22	cycle: every ingredient of the sections/sheafification proof route
23	(`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`, `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`,
24	`affineCoverSystem`, `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`) lives in a file that transitively imports

29	live in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` and are importable here.

46	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — reflecting `IsZero` through a faithful functor -/
47	
48	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
49	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then
50	`X` is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` +
51	`Functor.map_injective`; lighter than `reflects_exact_of_faithful`, which needs
52	`[Abelian]`.) Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory). -/
53	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
54	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [HasZeroMorphisms C] [HasZeroMorphisms D]
55	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
56	    (h : IsZero (F.obj Y)) : IsZero Y := by
57	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
58	  apply F.map_injective
59	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
60	  exact h
61	
62	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — a null-homotopic identity kills homology -/
63	
64	/-- **A complex homotopy-equivalent to zero has vanishing homology.**
65	If the identity of a homological complex `D` is homotopic to the zero map
66	(`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`), then every homology object `D.homology p` is a zero object.
67	This is the categorical packaging of the contracting-homotopy criterion: the
68	prepend-`i_fix` homotopy on the section {\v C}ech complex produces exactly such a
69	`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`, and this lemma converts it into the required
70	`IsZero (D.homology p)`.
71	
72	The proof is the standard three-lemma combo: homotopy-invariance of the induced
73	homology map (`Homotopy.homologyMap_eq`), the identity/zero computations
74	(`homologyMap_id`, `homologyMap_zero`), and the "identity equals zero" criterion for a
75	zero object (`IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero`).  Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological
76	algebra); blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`, formalization mechanism Step 3(d). -/
77	lemma isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
78	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (D : HomologicalComplex C c) (p : ι)
79	    [D.HasHomology p] (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) := by
80	  refine (IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero _).mpr ?_
81	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_id, ho.homologyMap_eq p, HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_zero]
82	
83	/-- **Transport `IsZero` of homology across a complex iso plus a null-homotopic identity.**
84	If `D ≅ D'` as homological complexes and the identity of `D'` is homotopic to `0`, then
85	`D.homology p` is a zero object.  This is the consumer-side glue for the section-{\v C}ech
86	route: the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech complex `D` is identified with a concrete section
87	complex `D'` (`cechSection_complex_iso`) which carries a contracting homotopy
88	(`cechSection_contractible`); this lemma turns that pair into `IsZero (D.homology p)`.
89	Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological algebra). -/
90	lemma isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
91	    [CategoryWithHomology C] {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι}
92	    {D D' : HomologicalComplex C c} (e : D ≅ D') (p : ι)
93	    (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) :=
94	  IsZero.of_iso (isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero D' p ho)
95	    ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor C c p).mapIso e)

97	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a locally-zero presheaf -/
98	
99	open Opposite in
100	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf that is locally a zero object vanishes.**
101	If for every object `U` of the site there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U` all of whose
102	members `g : V ⟶ U` land on an object `V` where `Q.obj (op V)` is a zero object, then
103	the sheafification of `Q` is the zero object.
104

114	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
115	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}

117	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → IsZero (Q.obj (op V))) :

124	  have hZ : IsZero Z := (Functor.isZero_iff Z).mpr hZobj
125	  haveI hli : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
126	    refine ⟨?_⟩
127	    intro X x y _
128	    obtain ⟨S, hS, hSzero⟩ := h X.unop
129	    apply J.superset_covering _ hS
130	    intro V g hg
131	    change (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) y

133	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
134	  haveI hls : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
135	    refine ⟨?_⟩
136	    intro U s
137	    refine J.superset_covering (fun V g _ => ?_) (J.top_mem U)
138	    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
139	    haveI : Subsingleton (Z.obj (op V)) := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hZobj (op V))
140	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
141	  exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective
142	    J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) hZ
143	
144	/-- **The evaluated augmented Čech section complex has vanishing homology over any open
145	contained in a cover member** (blueprint `lem:cechSection_isZero_homology`).
146	
147	For `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`, the augmented Čech complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F`, pushed
148	through the forgetful/restrict-scalars adapter and evaluated at `V`, has zero homology in
149	every degree.  This is the consumer-facing packaging of the Sub-brick A chain in
150	`CechSectionIdentification.lean`: the evaluated complex is identified with the concrete

152	prepend-`i` contracting homotopy (`cechSection_contractible`, valid since

154	combines the pair. -/

156	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) (p : ℕ) :
157	    let α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj
158	    let cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ
159	    let K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F
160	    let Kp := ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
161	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K
162	    let GV :=
163	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
164	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)
165	    IsZero (((GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj Kp).homology p) :=
166	  isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero (cechSection_complex_iso 𝒰 F V) p
167	    (cechSection_contractible 𝒰 F V i hiV)

72	### Subagent permissions
73	
74	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
75	
76	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
77	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
78	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
79	
80	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
81	
82	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper.
83	
84	## Protected Declarations
85	
86	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
87	
88	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
89	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
90	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
91	
92	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
93	
94	Two semantic markers:
95	
96	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
97	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
98	
99	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
100	
101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake build <module>` (it uses `lake build`, not `lake env lean <file>`, so dependencies are built and missing-import failures aren't spurious), and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents should NOT add or remove `\leanok` as routine bookkeeping — leave it to the sync. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).
102	
103	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
104	
105	## User Interaction
106	
107	The user provides hints in two places:
108	
109	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
124	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
125	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
127	- Curate `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` — the condensed, cross-iteration knowledge file injected (read-only) into every agent's prompt. Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
129	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
130	
131	### Prover Agent
132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
136	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
137	- A prover lane ends by writing its task result and exiting normally. **Do not kill Lean, Lake, shell, or sibling agent processes from inside the prover lane.** Do not run `pkill`, `killall`, regex-based process cleanup, or background-verification cleanup. If a verification command is still running, wait for its result or report that it is still running; process lifetime is owned by the Archon runner/orchestrator.
138	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
139	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
140	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
141	
142	### Subagents
143	
144	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
145	
146	### Review Agent
147	
148	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
149	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
150	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
151	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
152	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
153	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
154	
155	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
156	
157	These run automatically between phases:
158	
159	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
160	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
161	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

14	# The augmented Čech complex is exact (blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`)
15	
16	This file proves that the augmented Čech complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is exact
17	(every homology object is the zero object in `X.Modules`) whenever `𝒰` is a finite
18	affine open cover of `X` with all pairwise intersections affine (ensured by separatedness
19	of `X`) and `F` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module.
20	
21	The theorem is placed here rather than in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` to avoid an import
22	cycle: every ingredient of the sections/sheafification proof route
23	(`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`, `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`,
24	`affineCoverSystem`, `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`) lives in a file that transitively imports
25	`CechHigherDirectImage.lean`.  Placing `cechAugmented_exact` in this downstream file
26	(`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`) makes all those ingredients available without a cycle.
27	The pure-Mathlib site lemmas (`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W`,
28	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero`, `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`)
29	live in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` and are importable here.
30	
31	Blueprint: `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.
32	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
33	  `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`.
34	-/

46	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — reflecting `IsZero` through a faithful functor -/
47	
48	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
49	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then
50	`X` is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` +
51	`Functor.map_injective`; lighter than `reflects_exact_of_faithful`, which needs
52	`[Abelian]`.) Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory). -/
53	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
54	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [HasZeroMorphisms C] [HasZeroMorphisms D]
55	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
56	    (h : IsZero (F.obj Y)) : IsZero Y := by
57	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
58	  apply F.map_injective
59	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
60	  exact h
61	
62	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — a null-homotopic identity kills homology -/
63	
64	/-- **A complex homotopy-equivalent to zero has vanishing homology.**
65	If the identity of a homological complex `D` is homotopic to the zero map
66	(`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`), then every homology object `D.homology p` is a zero object.
67	This is the categorical packaging of the contracting-homotopy criterion: the
68	prepend-`i_fix` homotopy on the section {\v C}ech complex produces exactly such a
69	`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`, and this lemma converts it into the required
70	`IsZero (D.homology p)`.
71	
72	The proof is the standard three-lemma combo: homotopy-invariance of the induced
73	homology map (`Homotopy.homologyMap_eq`), the identity/zero computations
74	(`homologyMap_id`, `homologyMap_zero`), and the "identity equals zero" criterion for a
75	zero object (`IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero`).  Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological
76	algebra); blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`, formalization mechanism Step 3(d). -/
77	lemma isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
78	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (D : HomologicalComplex C c) (p : ι)
79	    [D.HasHomology p] (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) := by
80	  refine (IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero _).mpr ?_
81	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_id, ho.homologyMap_eq p, HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_zero]
82	
83	/-- **Transport `IsZero` of homology across a complex iso plus a null-homotopic identity.**
84	If `D ≅ D'` as homological complexes and the identity of `D'` is homotopic to `0`, then
85	`D.homology p` is a zero object.  This is the consumer-side glue for the section-{\v C}ech
86	route: the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech complex `D` is identified with a concrete section
87	complex `D'` (`cechSection_complex_iso`) which carries a contracting homotopy
88	(`cechSection_contractible`); this lemma turns that pair into `IsZero (D.homology p)`.
89	Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological algebra). -/
90	lemma isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
91	    [CategoryWithHomology C] {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι}
92	    {D D' : HomologicalComplex C c} (e : D ≅ D') (p : ι)
93	    (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) :=
94	  IsZero.of_iso (isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero D' p ho)
95	    ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor C c p).mapIso e)

97	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a locally-zero presheaf -/
98	
99	open Opposite in
100	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf that is locally a zero object vanishes.**
101	If for every object `U` of the site there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U` all of whose
102	members `g : V ⟶ U` land on an object `V` where `Q.obj (op V)` is a zero object, then
103	the sheafification of `Q` is the zero object.
104	
105	This packages the three imported site lemmas
106	(`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`) into the "locally zero" form the
107	{\v C}ech-resolution argument consumes: the presheaf homology `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V, F)` is a zero
108	object over every `V` contained in a cover member, and those `V` are a covering basis.
109	The local injectivity of `0 : Q ⟶ Z` (`Z` the constant zero presheaf) comes from the
110	hypothesis (each member's section group is a subsingleton); the local surjectivity is
111	free (the target is a zero object). Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
112	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C]
113	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
114	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
115	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}
116	    (h : ∀ U : C, ∃ S : Sieve U, S ∈ J U ∧
117	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → IsZero (Q.obj (op V))) :
118	    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}).obj Q) := by
119	  let Z : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} := (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
120	  have hZobj : ∀ x : Cᵒᵖ, IsZero (Z.obj x) := by
121	    intro x
122	    change IsZero (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
123	    exact AddCommGrpCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
124	  have hZ : IsZero Z := (Functor.isZero_iff Z).mpr hZobj
125	  haveI hli : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
126	    refine ⟨?_⟩
127	    intro X x y _
128	    obtain ⟨S, hS, hSzero⟩ := h X.unop
129	    apply J.superset_covering _ hS
130	    intro V g hg
131	    change (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) y
132	    haveI := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hSzero hg)
133	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
134	  haveI hls : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
135	    refine ⟨?_⟩
136	    intro U s
137	    refine J.superset_covering (fun V g _ => ?_) (J.top_mem U)
138	    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
139	    haveI : Subsingleton (Z.obj (op V)) := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hZobj (op V))
140	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
141	  exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective
142	    J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) hZ
143	
144	/-- **The evaluated augmented Čech section complex has vanishing homology over any open
145	contained in a cover member** (blueprint `lem:cechSection_isZero_homology`).
146	
147	For `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`, the augmented Čech complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F`, pushed
148	through the forgetful/restrict-scalars adapter and evaluated at `V`, has zero homology in
149	every degree.  This is the consumer-facing packaging of the Sub-brick A chain in
150	`CechSectionIdentification.lean`: the evaluated complex is identified with the concrete
151	augmented section Čech complex (`cechSection_complex_iso`), which carries the
152	prepend-`i` contracting homotopy (`cechSection_contractible`, valid since
153	`V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`); the in-file glue `isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero`
154	combines the pair. -/
155	lemma cechSection_isZero_homology (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
156	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) (p : ℕ) :
157	    let α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj
158	    let cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ
159	    let K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F
160	    let Kp := ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
161	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K
162	    let GV :=
163	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
164	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)
165	    IsZero (((GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj Kp).homology p) :=
166	  isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero (cechSection_complex_iso 𝒰 F V) p
167	    (cechSection_contractible 𝒰 F V i hiV)

169	/- Planner strategy:
170	Route: sections + sheafification (NOT stalks — `SheafOfModules.stalk` is absent from Mathlib).
171	Step 1: reflect `IsZero (homology p)` through the faithful additive forgetful functor
172	`SheafOfModules.toSheaf` (it preserves zero morphisms, so it reflects the zero object). Step 2:
173	the homology SHEAF = sheafification of the PRESHEAF homology, via the project engine
174	`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` (HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean). Step 3: the presheaf
175	homology is `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V,F)`, locally zero on the affine basis — over each basic affine `D(g) ⊆ Uᵢ`,
176	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` gives `F|_{D(g)} ≅ ~M` and
177	`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` (CechAcyclic.lean) kills positive-degree homology;
178	the basic affines are cofinal (`standard_cover_cofinal` / `affineCoverSystem`), so the map
179	`0 → presheaf-homology` is locally bijective, hence its sheafification (= the homology sheaf) is
180	zero. The reusable abelian-sheaf site lemmas `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W` /`_of_W_isZero`/
181	`_of_isLocallyBijective` (in CechHigherDirectImage.lean, importable) discharge the site-theory half.
182	The ONE bridge to build here: connect the module-level `homologyIsoSheafify` to those abelian-sheaf
183	site lemmas via the sheafification square `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ forget`
184	(cf. `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf`, used in AffineSerreVanishing.lean; see
185	`analogies/tosheaf-epi.md` and the iter-053 mathlib-analogist report on the toSheaf-reflection
186	bridge). Diamond-prone — work with `.hom` not `.val`, defeq/`change` not `rw`. Step 4: the
187	degree-0 augmentation node uses the same spanning-family exactness (`exact_of_isLocalized_span` /
188	`combDifferential_exact`). Blueprint: `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.
189	-/
190	
191	/-- **The augmented Čech complex is a resolution** (Stacks
192	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`; blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`).
193	
194	Let `X` be a separated scheme, `𝒰` a finite open cover of `X` all of whose members are
195	affine (so every finite intersection `U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{iₚ}` is affine by separatedness),
196	and `F` a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. Then every homology object of the augmented Čech
197	complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is zero:
198	```
199	  ∀ p, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology p).
200	```
201	Equivalently, the {\v C}ech nerve of `𝒰` is a resolution of `F` in `X.Modules`. -/
202	theorem cechAugmented_exact (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
203	    (h𝒰 : ∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)) [X.IsSeparated]
204	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
205	    ∀ p, IsZero ((cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).homology p) := by
206	  intro p
207	  -- Step 1: reflect through the faithful, zero-preserving `toSheaf`.
208	  apply isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf)
209	  -- Step 2: transport `IsZero` across `homologyIsoSheafify` and the sheafification square.
210	  set α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
211	  set cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ with hcc
212	  set K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F with hK
213	  set P := ((((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
214	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K).homology p) with hP
215	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
216	    ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso
217	        (PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify α cc K p) ≪≫
218	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
219	  -- Step 3: the presheaf homology is locally a zero object on the cover-member basis.
220	  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
221	  apply isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Q := Q)
222	  -- Section-level vanishing on every open `V` contained in a cover member `Uᵢ`:
223	  -- the restricted cover `{Uₛ ∩ V}` has the member `Uᵢ ∩ V = V`, giving a contracting
224	  -- (prepend-`i_fix`) homotopy on the augmented section complex over `V`. (F-agnostic.)
225	  have hSec : ∀ (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (i : 𝒰.I₀),
226	      V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i → IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
227	    intro V i hiV
228	    -- Evaluate-at-`V` the underlying presheaf complex: this functor is additive and
229	    -- preserves homology, so `Q.obj (op V) ≅ Hᵖ(Γ(V, K•))` (the section complex homology).
230	    set GV := PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
231	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V) with hGV
232	    set Kp := (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
233	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K) with hKp
234	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ (GV.mapHomologyIso' cc Kp p).symm
235	    -- Remaining: the augmented section complex `Γ(V, cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F)` is exact
236	    -- in degree `p`.  Since `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`, the restricted cover `{Uₛ ∩ V}` has the
237	    -- member `coverOpen 𝒰 i ∩ V = V`; prepending that fixed index `i` is a contracting
238	    -- homotopy `d∘h + h∘d = id` on the section complex (template
239	    -- `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` / the objectwise homotopy of FreePresheafComplex),
240	    -- so every homology object vanishes.  F-agnostic, cover-agnostic.
241	    -- Step 3(d): discharged by `cechSection_isZero_homology` above (the Sub-brick A chain
242	    -- of `CechSectionIdentification.lean`, packaged via `isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero`).
243	    exact cechSection_isZero_homology 𝒰 F V i hiV p
244	  -- Assemble the covering sieve `{g : V ⟶ U | V ≤ some Uᵢ}` of every open `U`.
245	  intro U
246	  refine ⟨{ arrows := fun {V} _ => ∃ i, V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i,
247	            downward_closed := fun {V W} g hg h => ?_ }, ?_, ?_⟩
248	  · obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := hg
249	    exact ⟨i, le_trans (leOfHom h) hi⟩
250	  · -- membership in the opens topology: cover `U` pointwise via `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ = ⊤`.
251	    intro x hx
252	    have hx' : x ∈ (⨆ i, coverOpen 𝒰 i) := by
253	      have htop : (⨆ i, coverOpen 𝒰 i) = ⊤ := Scheme.OpenCover.iSup_opensRange 𝒰
254	      rw [htop]; trivial
255	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp hx'
256	    exact ⟨U ⊓ coverOpen 𝒰 i, homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, inf_le_right⟩, hx, hi⟩
257	  · rintro V g ⟨i, hi⟩
258	    exact hSec V i hi
